sections.paragraph:
Βιθυνία, ας, ἡ (X. et al.; ins; Philo, Leg. ad Gai. 281; Joseph.) Bithynia, province in northern Asia Minor Ac 16:7; 1 Pt 1:1; JWeiss, RE X 553f (lit.).—RAC II 416–22; Kl. Pauly I 908–11.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

RE RE = Realencyclopädie für protestantische Theologie und Kirche—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 176
